article_meta.title: ‘President Trump,” reports NPR, “says he hasn’t decided whether he’ll launch a military strike on Iran.” This is a remar

reasoning: The summary maintains neutral language and attributes evaluations to the author, avoiding the article’s loaded rhetoric. A couple of key facts state the author’s claims as facts without explicit attribution, but overall framing remains measured and more neutral than the source.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs like ‘critiques,’ ‘argues,’ ‘notes,’ and ‘expresses concern,’ avoiding loaded phrasing.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Two items (lack of congressional debate; trend since Truman) are presented as facts without explicit attribution to the author.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Mostly attributed to the author; minor instances in key facts not explicitly labeled as opinion.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No heightened or alarming language; tone is measured.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks or character judgments.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about internal motives or thoughts.
custom_reasoning.slant: Reflects the article’s single-perspective critique proportionally; no added imbalance.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Omits the author’s brief acknowledgment of possible past justifications, but no distinct perspective is missing.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Does not amplify negative framing beyond the article’s content.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No preferential deference to elites or grassroots; describes institutional roles neutrally.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Strips away the article’s emotive and moralizing rhetoric and summarizes the argument neutrally.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Captures the author’s viewpoint and references NPR’s report; limited by the source’s one-sidedness but proportionate.
